exercises.translation: I must take medicine every day.
wrong_choice_explanations: なくてもいい means 'don't have to'; the opposite of obligation.
てはいけない means 'must not'; prohibition, not obligation.
たほうがいい means 'should'; a suggestion, not a strong obligation.

exercises.translation: I have to submit my homework.
wrong_choice_explanations: なくてもいい means 'don't need to submit'; opposite of must.
てはいけない means 'must not submit'; prohibition.
たい expresses desire ('want to'), not obligation.

exercises.translation: I must get up early tomorrow.
wrong_choice_explanations: なくてもいい means 'don't have to get up early'; no obligation.
てもいい means 'it's okay to get up early'; permission, not obligation.
たほうがいい means 'it would be better to'; advice, not necessity.

exercises.example_sentence: パスポートを持ってい＿＿。
wrong_choice_explanations: なくてもいい means 'don't need to have it'; no obligation.
てはいけない means 'must not have it'; prohibition.
てもいい means 'it's okay to have it'; permission, not requirement.

exercises.translation: I have to study before the exam.
wrong_choice_explanations: なくてもいい means 'don't have to study'; no obligation.
てはいけない means 'must not study'; prohibition.
たい means 'want to study'; desire, not necessity.

exercises.example_sentence: このレポートは今日中に書か＿＿。
exercises.translation: This report must be written by the end of today.
wrong_choice_explanations: なくてもいい means 'doesn't have to be written'; no obligation.
てもいい means 'it's okay to write'; permission, not obligation.
たほうがいい means 'it would be better to write'; suggestion, not must.
なくてもいい means 'don't have to attend'; optional.
てはいけない means 'must not attend'; prohibition.
たい means 'want to attend'; desire, not obligation.
なくてもいい means 'don't have to follow'; no obligation.
てもいい means 'it's okay to follow'; permission, not must.
てはいけない means 'must not follow'; prohibition.

exercises.translation: I must apply for a visa.
wrong_choice_explanations: なくてもいい means 'don't need to apply'; optional.
てはいけない means 'must not apply'; prohibition.
たほうがいい means 'should apply'; advice, weaker than must.

exercises.translation: You must not drive unless you are 18.
wrong_choice_explanations: なければならない means 'must drive'; opposite of the intended prohibition.
てもいい means 'it's okay to drive'; permission, not prohibition.
たい means 'want to drive'; desire, unrelated to the rule.
